questions.html: If the integral ∫bxcos4x-asin4xx2dx=asin4xx+c , then the values of a and b are:
questions.questionHtml: If the integral ∫bxcos4x-asin4xx2dx=asin4xx+c , then the values of a and b are:
questions.solutionText: Solution I = ∫ b x cos 4 x - a sin 4 x x 2 d x By integration by parts = b x cos 4 x - a sin 4 x × - 1 x + ∫ b cos 4 x - 4 b x sin 4 x - 4 a cos 4 x x d x + k = - b x cos 4 x - a sin 4 x x + ∫ b - 4 a x cos 4 x - 4 b sin 4 x d x + k = - b cos 4 x + a sin 4 x x + b cos 4 x + ∫ b - 4 a x cos 4 x d x + c Given, I = a sin 4 x x + c By comparing we can say, b - 4 a = 0 ⇒ b = 4 a Hence, we can write a = 1 & b = 4
questions.solution: Solution I = ∫ b x cos 4 x - a sin 4 x x 2 d x By integration by parts = b x cos 4 x - a sin 4 x × - 1 x + ∫ b cos 4 x - 4 b x sin 4 x - 4 a cos 4 x x d x + k = - b x cos 4 x - a sin 4 x x + ∫ b - 4 a x cos 4 x - 4 b sin 4 x d x + k = - b cos 4 x + a sin 4 x x + b cos 4 x + ∫ b - 4 a x cos 4 x d x + c Given, I = a sin 4 x x + c By comparing we can say, b - 4 a = 0 ⇒ b = 4 a Hence, we can write a = 1 & b = 4
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
I=∫bxcos4x-asin4xx2dx

By integration by parts

=bxcos4x-asin4x×-1x+∫bcos4x-4bxsin4x-4acos4xxdx+k

=-bxcos4x-asin4xx+∫b-4axcos4x-4bsin4xdx+k

=-bcos4x+asin4xx+bcos4x+∫b-4axcos4xdx+c

Given,

I=asin4xx+c

By comparing we can say, b-4a=0⇒b=4a

Hence, we can write a=1 & b=4

questions.questionHtml: There are p+q different books on different topics in Mathematics, where p≠q. If L= The number of ways in which these books are distributed between two students X and Y such that X get p books and Y gets q books. M= The number of ways in which these books are distributed between two students X and Y such that one of them gets p books and another gets q books. N = The number of ways in which these books are divided into two groups of p books and q books, then
questions.solutionText: Solution For calculating L , first select p books out of given ( p + q ) books and give to X and then give the remaining q books to Y ⇒ L = C p p + q C q q = C p p + q For calculating M , consider two cases based on who gets p number of books i X gets p books and Y gets q books ⇒ M 1 = L = C p p + q ii Y gets p books and X gets p books this case is similar to i ⇒ M 2 = M 1 = C p p + q ⇒ M = M 1 + M 2 = 2 · C p p + q = 2 L For calculating N , select p books out of p + q books and form a group. The remaining q books will form the second group eventually. ⇒ N = Number of ways of selecting p books out of p + q = C p p + q = L ⇒ L = M 2 = N ⇒ 2 L = M = 2 N
questions.solution: Solution For calculating L , first select p books out of given ( p + q ) books and give to X and then give the remaining q books to Y ⇒ L = C p p + q C q q = C p p + q For calculating M , consider two cases based on who gets p number of books i X gets p books and Y gets q books ⇒ M 1 = L = C p p + q ii Y gets p books and X gets p books this case is similar to i ⇒ M 2 = M 1 = C p p + q ⇒ M = M 1 + M 2 = 2 · C p p + q = 2 L For calculating N , select p books out of p + q books and form a group. The remaining q books will form the second group eventually. ⇒ N = Number of ways of selecting p books out of p + q = C p p + q = L ⇒ L = M 2 = N ⇒ 2 L = M = 2 N
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
For calculating L , first select p books out of given(p+q) books and give to X and then give the remaining q books to Y
⇒ L=Cp p+qCq q=Cp p+q
For calculating M , consider two cases based on who gets p number of books
i X gets p books and Y gets q books
⇒ M1=L=Cp p+q
ii Y gets p books and X gets p books this case is similar to i
⇒M2=M1=Cpp+q
⇒ M=M1+M2=2·Cp p+q=2L
For calculating N , select p books out of p+q books and form a group. The remaining q books will form the second group eventually.
⇒ N= Number of ways of selecting p books out of p+q=Cpp+q=L
⇒ L=M2=N
⇒ 2L=M=2N

questions.text: Let f ( x ) = | | x | − 1 | + | x | . The number of points in the interval ( − ∞ , ∞ ) where the function f ( x ) is not differentiable is:
questions.questionText: Let f ( x ) = | | x | − 1 | + | x | . The number of points in the interval ( − ∞ , ∞ ) where the function f ( x ) is not differentiable is:
questions.question: Let f ( x ) = | | x | − 1 | + | x | . The number of points in the interval ( − ∞ , ∞ ) where the function f ( x ) is not differentiable is:
questions.prompt: Let f ( x ) = | | x | − 1 | + | x | . The number of points in the interval ( − ∞ , ∞ ) where the function f ( x ) is not differentiable is:
questions.html: Let f(x)=||x|−1|+|x|. The number of points in the interval ( −∞,∞ ) where the function f(x) is not differentiable is:
questions.questionHtml: Let f(x)=||x|−1|+|x|. The number of points in the interval ( −∞,∞ ) where the function f(x) is not differentiable is:
questions.solutionText: Solution f ( x ) = | | x | − 1 | + | x | Candidate points: x = 0 , ± 1 Case 1: x ∈ ( − 1 , 1 ) : | x | < 1 ⇒ | | x | − 1 | = 1 − | x | f ( x ) = ( 1 − | x | ) + | x | = 1 (constant) ⇒ differentiable at x = 0 Case 2: x ∈ ( 1 , ∞ ) : | x | > 1 ⇒ | | x | − 1 | = x − 1 f ( x ) = ( x − 1 ) + x = 2 x − 1 ⇒ slope = 2 Case 3: x ∈ ( − ∞ , − 1 ) : | x | > 1 ⇒ | | x | − 1 | = − x − 1 f ( x ) = ( − x − 1 ) + ( − x ) = − 2 x − 1 ⇒ slope = − 2 At x = 1 : left slope (from f = 1 ) = 0 , right slope = 2 ⇒ not differentiable. At x = − 1 : left slope = − 2 , right slope (from f = 1 ) = 0 ⇒ not differentiable. At x = 0 : f ( x ) = 1 on both sides ⇒ differentiable. Points of non-differentiability: x = 1 and x = − 1 only. Answer: (B) 2
questions.solution: Solution f ( x ) = | | x | − 1 | + | x | Candidate points: x = 0 , ± 1 Case 1: x ∈ ( − 1 , 1 ) : | x | < 1 ⇒ | | x | − 1 | = 1 − | x | f ( x ) = ( 1 − | x | ) + | x | = 1 (constant) ⇒ differentiable at x = 0 Case 2: x ∈ ( 1 , ∞ ) : | x | > 1 ⇒ | | x | − 1 | = x − 1 f ( x ) = ( x − 1 ) + x = 2 x − 1 ⇒ slope = 2 Case 3: x ∈ ( − ∞ , − 1 ) : | x | > 1 ⇒ | | x | − 1 | = − x − 1 f ( x ) = ( − x − 1 ) + ( − x ) = − 2 x − 1 ⇒ slope = − 2 At x = 1 : left slope (from f = 1 ) = 0 , right slope = 2 ⇒ not differentiable. At x = − 1 : left slope = − 2 , right slope (from f = 1 ) = 0 ⇒ not differentiable. At x = 0 : f ( x ) = 1 on both sides ⇒ differentiable. Points of non-differentiability: x = 1 and x = − 1 only. Answer: (B) 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
f(x)=||x|−1|+|x|

Candidate points: x=0,±1

Case 1: x∈(−1,1): |x|<1⇒||x|−1|=1−|x|
f(x)=(1−|x|)+|x|=1 (constant) ⇒ differentiable at x=0

Case 2: x∈(1,∞): |x|>1⇒||x|−1|=x−1
f(x)=(x−1)+x=2x−1 ⇒ slope =2

Case 3: x∈(−∞,−1): |x|>1⇒||x|−1|=−x−1
f(x)=(−x−1)+(−x)=−2x−1 ⇒ slope =−2

At x=1: left slope (from f=1) =0, right slope =2 ⇒ not differentiable.
At x=−1: left slope =−2, right slope (from f=1) =0 ⇒ not differentiable.
At x=0: f(x)=1 on both sides ⇒ differentiable.

Points of non-differentiability: x=1 and x=−1 only.

Answer: (B) 2

questions.prompt: The value of cos ⁡ π 4 + i sin ⁡ π 4 6 is equal to -
questions.html: The value of cos⁡π4+isin⁡π46 is equal to -
questions.questionHtml: The value of cos⁡π4+isin⁡π46 is equal to -
questions.solutionText: Solution Let E = cos ⁡ π 4 + i sin ⁡ π 4 6 ⇒ E = cos 6 π 4 + i sin 6 π 4 (By De-Moivre's Theorem) ⇒ E = cos 3 π 2 + i sin 3 π 2 ⇒ E = 0 + i - 1 ⇒ E = - i .
questions.solution: Solution Let E = cos ⁡ π 4 + i sin ⁡ π 4 6 ⇒ E = cos 6 π 4 + i sin 6 π 4 (By De-Moivre's Theorem) ⇒ E = cos 3 π 2 + i sin 3 π 2 ⇒ E = 0 + i - 1 ⇒ E = - i .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Let E=cos⁡π4+isin⁡π46

⇒E=cos6π4+isin6π4 (By De-Moivre's Theorem)

⇒E=cos3π2+isin3π2

⇒E=0+i-1

⇒E=-i.

questions.questionHtml: The sum of the series 11+12+14+21+22+24+31+32+34+……… to n terms is
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let Tn be the nth term of the series
11+12+14+21+22+24+31+32+34+……
Then Tn=n1+n2+n4=n(1+n2)2−n2
=n(n2+n+1)(n2−n+1)=12[1n2−n+1−1n2+n+1]=12[11+(n−1)n−11+n(n+1)]
Now ∑r=1nTr=12[11−11+1.2]+12[11+1.2−11+2.3]
+12[11+2.3−11+3.4]+…….+12[11+(n−1)n−11+n(n+1)]=12[1−11+n(n+1)]=n(n+1)2(n2+n+1).
Trick: Checking for n=1,2.S1=13 and S2=37 which are given by (b).

questions.questionText: A straight line segment of fixed length L slides with its endpoints A and B staying on the x axis and y -axis respectively. questions.prompt: A straight line segment of fixed length L slides with its endpoints A and B staying on the x axis and y -axis respectively. A point P lies on the segment A B such that it divides A B in the ratio 1 : 2 (where the " 1 " part is adjacent to the x -axis). The locus of point P is:
questions.html: A straight line segment of fixed length L slides with its endpoints A and B staying on the x axis and y-axis respectively. A point P lies on the segment AB such that it divides AB in the ratio 1:2 (where the " 1 " part is adjacent to the x-axis). The locus of point P is:
questions.questionHtml: A straight line segment of fixed length L slides with its endpoints A and B staying on the x axis and y-axis respectively. A point P lies on the segment AB such that it divides AB in the ratio 1:2 (where the " 1 " part is adjacent to the x-axis). The locus of point P is:
questions.solutionText: Solution Let A = ( a , 0 ) on x-axis and B = ( 0 , b ) on y-axis, with A B = L ⇒ a 2 + b 2 = L 2 . P divides A B in ratio 1 : 2 (from A-side, i.e., closer to x-axis): P = ( 1 ⋅ 0 + 2 ⋅ a 3 , 1 ⋅ b + 2 ⋅ 0 3 ) = ( 2 a 3 , b 3 ) Let P = ( h , k ) : h = 2 a 3 ⇒ a = 3 h 2 k = b 3 ⇒ b = 3 k Substituting in a 2 + b 2 = L 2 : 9 h 2 4 + 9 k 2 = L 2 Multiply by 4: 9 h 2 + 36 k 2 = 4 L 2 Answer: A) 9 x 2 + 36 y 2 = 4 L 2
questions.solution: Solution Let A = ( a , 0 ) on x-axis and B = ( 0 , b ) on y-axis, with A B = L ⇒ a 2 + b 2 = L 2 . P divides A B in ratio 1 : 2 (from A-side, i.e., closer to x-axis): P = ( 1 ⋅ 0 + 2 ⋅ a 3 , 1 ⋅ b + 2 ⋅ 0 3 ) = ( 2 a 3 , b 3 ) Let P = ( h , k ) : h = 2 a 3 ⇒ a = 3 h 2 k = b 3 ⇒ b = 3 k Substituting in a 2 + b 2 = L 2 : 9 h 2 4 + 9 k 2 = L 2 Multiply by 4: 9 h 2 + 36 k 2 = 4 L 2 Answer: A) 9 x 2 + 36 y 2 = 4 L 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let A=(a,0) on x-axis and B=(0,b) on y-axis, with AB=L⇒a2+b2=L2.

P divides AB in ratio 1:2 (from A-side, i.e., closer to x-axis):
P=(1⋅0+2⋅a3, 1⋅b+2⋅03)=(2a3, b3)

Let P=(h,k):
h=2a3⇒a=3h2
k=b3⇒b=3k

Substituting in a2+b2=L2:
9h24+9k2=L2

Multiply by 4:
9h2+36k2=4L2

Answer: A) 9x2+36y2=4L2

questions.html: If A=102021203 is a root of polynomial x3-6x2+7x+k=0, then the value of k is
questions.questionHtml: If A=102021203 is a root of polynomial x3-6x2+7x+k=0, then the value of k is
questions.solutionText: Solution Since, we know the matrix, we can find the characteristics polynomial of this matrix, p λ when λ are the eigenvalue of the matrix which are the roots of polynomial. So, p λ = det A - λ I , I is the identity matrix. A - λ I = 1 0 2 0 2 1 2 0 3 - λ 0 0 0 λ 0 0 0 λ = 1 - λ 0 2 0 2 - λ 1 2 0 3 - λ So, det A - λ I = 1 - λ 2 - λ 3 - λ - 0 + 2 - 2 × 2 - λ = ( 1 − λ ) 6 − 5 λ + λ 2 + 0 + 2 ( − 4 + 2 λ ) = 6 − 5 λ + λ 2 − 6 λ + 5 λ 2 − λ 3 − 8 + 4 λ = − λ 3 + 6 λ 2 − 7 λ − 2 We see, that the characteristic polynomial is same as the polynomial given. If we compare both, k = 2 .
questions.solution: Solution Since, we know the matrix, we can find the characteristics polynomial of this matrix, p λ when λ are the eigenvalue of the matrix which are the roots of polynomial. So, p λ = det A - λ I , I is the identity matrix. A - λ I = 1 0 2 0 2 1 2 0 3 - λ 0 0 0 λ 0 0 0 λ = 1 - λ 0 2 0 2 - λ 1 2 0 3 - λ So, det A - λ I = 1 - λ 2 - λ 3 - λ - 0 + 2 - 2 × 2 - λ = ( 1 − λ ) 6 − 5 λ + λ 2 + 0 + 2 ( − 4 + 2 λ ) = 6 − 5 λ + λ 2 − 6 λ + 5 λ 2 − λ 3 − 8 + 4 λ = − λ 3 + 6 λ 2 − 7 λ − 2 We see, that the characteristic polynomial is same as the polynomial given. If we compare both, k = 2 .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Since, we know the matrix, we can find the characteristics polynomial of this matrix, pλ when λ are the eigenvalue of the matrix which are the roots of polynomial.

So, pλ=detA-λI, I is the identity matrix.

A-λI=102021203-λ000λ000λ=1-λ0202-λ1203-λ

So, detA-λI

=1-λ2-λ3-λ-0+2-2×2-λ

=(1−λ)6−5λ+λ2+0+2(−4+2λ)

=6−5λ+λ2−6λ+5λ2−λ3−8+4λ

=−λ3+6λ2−7λ−2

We see, that the characteristic polynomial is same as the polynomial given. If we compare both, k=2.

questions.questionText: Two parabolas with a common vertex and with axes along the x -axis and y -axis respectively, intersect each other in the first quadrant. If the length of the latus rectum of each parabola is 3 , then the equation of the common tangent to the two parabolas is :
questions.question: Two parabolas with a common vertex and with axes along the x -axis and y -axis respectively, intersect each other in the first quadrant. If the length of the latus rectum of each parabola is 3 , then the equation of the common tangent to the two parabolas is :
questions.prompt: Two parabolas with a common vertex and with axes along the x -axis and y -axis respectively, intersect each other in the first quadrant. If the length of the latus rectum of each parabola is 3 , then the equation of the common tangent to the two parabolas is :
questions.html: Two parabolas with a common vertex and with axes along the x-axis and y-axis respectively, intersect each other in the first quadrant. If the length of the latus rectum of each parabola is 3, then the equation of the common tangent to the two parabolas is :
questions.questionHtml: Two parabolas with a common vertex and with axes along the x-axis and y-axis respectively, intersect each other in the first quadrant. If the length of the latus rectum of each parabola is 3, then the equation of the common tangent to the two parabolas is :
questions.solutionText: Solution Equation of two parabola are y 2 = 3 x and x 2 = 3 y . Let equation of tangent to y 2 = 3 x is y = m x + 3 4 m is also tangent to x 2 = 3 y ⇒ x 2 = 3 m x + 9 4 m ⇒ 4 m x 2 - 12 m 2 x - 9 = 0 have equal roots ⇒ D = 0 ⇒ 144 m 4 = 4 4 m ( - 9 ) ⇒ m 4 + m = 0 ⇒ m = - 1 Hence, common tangent is y = - x - 3 4 ⇒ 4 x + y + 3 = 0
questions.solution: Solution Equation of two parabola are y 2 = 3 x and x 2 = 3 y . Let equation of tangent to y 2 = 3 x is y = m x + 3 4 m is also tangent to x 2 = 3 y ⇒ x 2 = 3 m x + 9 4 m ⇒ 4 m x 2 - 12 m 2 x - 9 = 0 have equal roots ⇒ D = 0 ⇒ 144 m 4 = 4 4 m ( - 9 ) ⇒ m 4 + m = 0 ⇒ m = - 1 Hence, common tangent is y = - x - 3 4 ⇒ 4 x + y + 3 = 0
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Equation of two parabola are y2=3x and x2=3y.

Let equation of tangent to y2=3x is y=mx+34m is also tangent to x2=3y

⇒ x2=3mx+94m

⇒4mx2-12m2x-9=0 have equal roots

⇒D=0

⇒144m4=44m(-9)

⇒m4+m=0⇒m=-1

Hence, common tangent is y=-x-34

⇒4x+y+3=0

questions.solutionText: Solution The given lines are x − 1 − 3 = y − 2 − 2 k = z − 3 2 . . . . ( 1 ) x − 1 k = y − 2 1 = z − 3 5 . . . . . . . ( 2 ) For ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) to be ⊥ , we must have − 3 k − 2 k + 10 = 0 ⇒ − 5 k + 10 = 0 ⇒ k = 2. The equation of lines becomes x − 1 − 3 = y − 2 − 4 = z − 3 2 , x − 1 2 = y − 2 1 = z − 3 5 Now, the equation of plane containing these two lines is x - x 1 y - y 1 z - z 1 l 1 m 1 n 1 l 2 m 2 n 2 = 0 where x 1 = 1 , y 1 = 2 , z 1 = 3 , l 1 = − 3 , m 1 = − 4 , n 1 = 2 l 2 = 2 , m 2 = 1 , n 2 = 5 ∵ x - 1 y - 2 z - 3 - 3 - 4 2 2 1 5 = 0 ⇒ x − 1 − 20 − 2 − y − 2 − 15 − 4 + z − 3 − 3 + 8 = 0 ⇒ − 22 x + 22 + 19 y − 38 + 5 z − 15 = 0 ⇒ 22 x − 19 y − 5 z + 31 = 0 .
questions.solution: Solution The given lines are x − 1 − 3 = y − 2 − 2 k = z − 3 2 . . . . ( 1 ) x − 1 k = y − 2 1 = z − 3 5 . . . . . . . ( 2 ) For ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) to be ⊥ , we must have − 3 k − 2 k + 10 = 0 ⇒ − 5 k + 10 = 0 ⇒ k = 2. The equation of lines becomes x − 1 − 3 = y − 2 − 4 = z − 3 2 , x − 1 2 = y − 2 1 = z − 3 5 Now, the equation of plane containing these two lines is x - x 1 y - y 1 z - z 1 l 1 m 1 n 1 l 2 m 2 n 2 = 0 where x 1 = 1 , y 1 = 2 , z 1 = 3 , l 1 = − 3 , m 1 = − 4 , n 1 = 2 l 2 = 2 , m 2 = 1 , n 2 = 5 ∵ x - 1 y - 2 z - 3 - 3 - 4 2 2 1 5 = 0 ⇒ x − 1 − 20 − 2 − y − 2 − 15 − 4 + z − 3 − 3 + 8 = 0 ⇒ − 22 x + 22 + 19 y − 38 + 5 z − 15 = 0 ⇒ 22 x − 19 y − 5 z + 31 = 0 .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The given lines are

x−1−3=y−2−2k=z−32....(1)

x−1k=y−21=z−35.......(2)

For (1) and (2) to be ⊥, we must have −3k−2k +10=0

⇒−5k+10=0

⇒k=2.

The equation of lines becomes

x−1−3=y−2−4=z−32,

x−12=y−21=z−35

Now, the equation of plane containing these two lines is

x-x1y-y1z-z1l1m1n1l2m2n2=0

where x1=1, y1=2, z1=3,

l1=−3, m1=−4, n1=2

l2=2, m2=1, n2=5

∵x-1y-2z-3-3-42215=0

⇒x−1−20−2−y−2−15−4+z−3−3+8=0

⇒−22x+22+19y−38+5z−15=0

⇒22x−19y−5z+31=0.

questions.html:
The age distribution of workers in a factory is as follows:
Age in Years No. of Workers
20-28 45
28-36 95
36-44 100
44-52 42
52-60 18

If 15% of the total strength starting from the lowest age group is retrenched and 20% of the total strength from the highest age groups is given premature retirement, then the range of age of workers retained in the factory is
questions.questionHtml:
The age distribution of workers in a factory is as follows:
Age in Years No. of Workers
20-28 45
28-36 95
36-44 100
44-52 42
52-60 18

If 15% of the total strength starting from the lowest age group is retrenched and 20% of the total strength from the highest age groups is given premature retirement, then the range of age of workers retained in the factory is
questions.solutionText: Solution From the given table, the total number of workers = 300 Retrenched = 15 % of 300 = 45 These are all from age group 20 - 28 . (Given) Premature retired = 20 % of 300 = 60 So, 18 workers from age group 52 - 60 and 42 workers from age group ( 44 - 52 ) . ∴ The age limit of workers retained is 28 - 44 .

questions.text: All the letters of the word PUBLIC are written in all possible orders and these words are written as in a dictionary with serial numbers. Then the serial number of the word PUBLIC is

questions.html: All the letters of the word PUBLIC are written in all possible orders and these words are written as in a dictionary with serial numbers. Then the serial number of the word PUBLIC is
questions.questionHtml: All the letters of the word PUBLIC are written in all possible orders and these words are written as in a dictionary with serial numbers. Then the serial number of the word PUBLIC is
questions.solutionText: Solution The given word is PUBLIC Arranging the letters alphabetically, we get BCILPU When the word starts with any of the letters B/C/I/L, the number of possibilities = 5 ! × 4 = 480 Now when the word starts with PB, then the number of possibilities = 4 ! = 24 Now when the word starts with PC, then the number of possibilities = 4 ! = 24 Now when the word starts with PI, then the number of possibilities = 4 ! = 24 Now when the word starts with PL, then the number of possibilities = 4 ! = 24 Now when the word starts with PUBC, then the number of possibilities = 2 ! = 2 Now when the word starts with PUBI, then the number of possibilities = 2 ! = 2 Now when the word starts with PUBLC, then the number of possibilities = 1 Now when the word starts with PUBLIC, then the number of possibilities = 1 Rank = 480 + 24 × 4 + 2 × 2 + 1 × 2 = 582 Hence, rank of the work PUBLIC is 582 .
questions.solution: Solution The given word is PUBLIC Arranging the letters alphabetically, we get BCILPU When the word starts with any of the letters B/C/I/L, the number of possibilities = 5 ! × 4 = 480 Now when the word starts with PB, then the number of possibilities = 4 ! = 24 Now when the word starts with PC, then the number of possibilities = 4 ! = 24 Now when the word starts with PI, then the number of possibilities = 4 ! = 24 Now when the word starts with PL, then the number of possibilities = 4 ! = 24 Now when the word starts with PUBC, then the number of possibilities = 2 ! = 2 Now when the word starts with PUBI, then the number of possibilities = 2 ! = 2 Now when the word starts with PUBLC, then the number of possibilities = 1 Now when the word starts with PUBLIC, then the number of possibilities = 1 Rank = 480 + 24 × 4 + 2 × 2 + 1 × 2 = 582 Hence, rank of the work PUBLIC is 582 .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

The given word is PUBLIC

Arranging the letters alphabetically, we get

BCILPU

When the word starts with any of the letters B/C/I/L, the number of possibilities=5!×4=480

Now when the word starts with PB, then the number of possibilities=4!=24

Now when the word starts with PC, then the number of possibilities =4!=24

Now when the word starts with PI, then the number of possibilities =4!=24

Now when the word starts with PL, then the number of possibilities =4!=24

Now when the word starts with PUBC, then the number of possibilities=2!=2

Now when the word starts with PUBI, then the number of possibilities =2!=2

Now when the word starts with PUBLC, then the number of possibilities=1

Now when the word starts with PUBLIC, then the number of possibilities =1

Rank =480+24×4+2×2+1×2=582

Hence, rank of the work PUBLIC is 582.

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
a→=i^+5k^, b→=2i^+3k^, c→=4i^−j^+2k^, d→=i^−j^

c→−a→=(4−1)i^+(−1)j^+(2−5)k^=3i^−j^−3k^

b→×d→=|i^j^k^2031−10|
=i^(0⋅0−3⋅(−1))−j^(2⋅0−3⋅1)+k^(2⋅(−1)−0⋅1)
=i^(3)−j^(−3)+k^(−2)
=3i^+3j^−2k^

(c→−a→)⋅(b→×d→)=(3)(3)+(−1)(3)+(−3)(−2)
=9−3+6=12

Answer: Option (1) 12

questions.solutionText: Solution Total balls: 2W + 3B + 4R = 9 Total ways to draw 3 balls = ( 9 3 ) = 84 Ways with NO black ball (only from 2W + 4R = 6 balls) = ( 6 3 ) = 20 Ways with at least one black ball = 84 − 20 = 64 Answer: Option (1) 64

Total balls: 2W + 3B + 4R = 9

Ways with NO black ball (only from 2W + 4R = 6 balls) =(63)=20

Answer: Option (1) 64

questions.text: The coordinates of the triangle inscribed in parabola y 2 = 4 a x are y 1 , y 2 , y 3 , then the area of triangle is
questions.questionText: The coordinates of the triangle inscribed in parabola y 2 = 4 a x are y 1 , y 2 , y 3 , then the area of triangle is
questions.question: The coordinates of the triangle inscribed in parabola y 2 = 4 a x are y 1 , y 2 , y 3 , then the area of triangle is
questions.prompt: The coordinates of the triangle inscribed in parabola y 2 = 4 a x are y 1 , y 2 , y 3 , then the area of triangle is
questions.html: The coordinates of the triangle inscribed in parabola y2=4ax are y1,y2,y3, then the area of triangle is
questions.questionHtml: The coordinates of the triangle inscribed in parabola y2=4ax are y1,y2,y3, then the area of triangle is
questions.solutionText: Solution Points on parabola y 2 = 4 a x : ( y i 2 4 a , y i ) for i = 1 , 2 , 3 . Area of triangle with vertices ( x 1 , y 1 ) , ( x 2 , y 2 ) , ( x 3 , y 3 ) : Δ = 1 2 | x 1 ( y 2 − y 3 ) + x 2 ( y 3 − y 1 ) + x 3 ( y 1 − y 2 ) | Substituting x i = y i 2 4 a : Δ = 1 2 | y 1 2 4 a ( y 2 − y 3 ) + y 2 2 4 a ( y 3 − y 1 ) + y 3 2 4 a ( y 1 − y 2 ) | = 1 8 a | y 1 2 ( y 2 − y 3 ) + y 2 2 ( y 3 − y 1 ) + y 3 2 ( y 1 − y 2 ) | Using the identity: y 1 2 ( y 2 − y 3 ) + y 2 2 ( y 3 − y 1 ) + y 3 2 ( y 1 − y 2 ) = ( y 1 − y 2 ) ( y 2 − y 3 ) ( y 3 − y 1 ) Δ = 1 8 a | ( y 1 − y 2 ) ( y 2 − y 3 ) ( y 3 − y 1 ) | Answer: Option (3) 1 8 a ( y 1 − y 2 ) ( y 2 − y 3 ) ( y 3 − y 1 )
questions.solution: Solution Points on parabola y 2 = 4 a x : ( y i 2 4 a , y i ) for i = 1 , 2 , 3 . Area of triangle with vertices ( x 1 , y 1 ) , ( x 2 , y 2 ) , ( x 3 , y 3 ) : Δ = 1 2 | x 1 ( y 2 − y 3 ) + x 2 ( y 3 − y 1 ) + x 3 ( y 1 − y 2 ) | Substituting x i = y i 2 4 a : Δ = 1 2 | y 1 2 4 a ( y 2 − y 3 ) + y 2 2 4 a ( y 3 − y 1 ) + y 3 2 4 a ( y 1 − y 2 ) | = 1 8 a | y 1 2 ( y 2 − y 3 ) + y 2 2 ( y 3 − y 1 ) + y 3 2 ( y 1 − y 2 ) | Using the identity: y 1 2 ( y 2 − y 3 ) + y 2 2 ( y 3 − y 1 ) + y 3 2 ( y 1 − y 2 ) = ( y 1 − y 2 ) ( y 2 − y 3 ) ( y 3 − y 1 ) Δ = 1 8 a | ( y 1 − y 2 ) ( y 2 − y 3 ) ( y 3 − y 1 ) | Answer: Option (3) 1 8 a ( y 1 − y 2 ) ( y 2 − y 3 ) ( y 3 − y 1 )
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Points on parabola y2=4ax: (yi24a,yi) for i=1,2,3.

Area of triangle with vertices (x1,y1),(x2,y2),(x3,y3):
Δ=12|x1(y2−y3)+x2(y3−y1)+x3(y1−y2)|

Substituting xi=yi24a:

Δ=12|y124a(y2−y3)+y224a(y3−y1)+y324a(y1−y2)|

=18a|y12(y2−y3)+y22(y3−y1)+y32(y1−y2)|

Using the identity: y12(y2−y3)+y22(y3−y1)+y32(y1−y2)=(y1−y2)(y2−y3)(y3−y1)

Δ=18a|(y1−y2)(y2−y3)(y3−y1)|

Answer: Option (3) 18a(y1−y2)(y2−y3)(y3−y1)

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Maximize the objective function Z = 3 x + 5 y subject to the following constraints: x + 2 y ≤ 20 x + y ≤ 15 y ≤ 8 x ≥ 0 , y ≥ 0 What is the maximum value of Z ?
questions.questionText: Maximize the objective function Z = 3 x + 5 y subject to the following constraints: x + 2 y ≤ 20 x + y ≤ 15 y ≤ 8 x ≥ 0 , y ≥ 0 What is the maximum value of Z ?
questions.question: Maximize the objective function Z = 3 x + 5 y subject to the following constraints: x + 2 y ≤ 20 x + y ≤ 15 y ≤ 8 x ≥ 0 , y ≥ 0 What is the maximum value of Z ?
questions.prompt: Maximize the objective function Z = 3 x + 5 y subject to the following constraints: x + 2 y ≤ 20 x + y ≤ 15 y ≤ 8 x ≥ 0 , y ≥ 0 What is the maximum value of Z ?
questions.html:
Maximize the objective function Z=3x+5y subject to the following constraints:

x+2y≤20
x+y≤15
y≤8
x≥0,y≥0

What is the maximum value of Z ?
questions.questionHtml:
Maximize the objective function Z=3x+5y subject to the following constraints:

x+2y≤20
x+y≤15
y≤8
x≥0,y≥0

What is the maximum value of Z ?
questions.solutionText: Solution Find corner points of the feasible region: Intersection of x + 2 y = 20 and x + y = 15 : subtract ⇒ y = 5 , x = 10 ⇒ ( 10 , 5 ) Intersection of x + y = 15 and y = 8 : x = 7 ⇒ ( 7 , 8 ) — check feasibility: x + 2 y = 7 + 16 = 23 > 20 ⇒ infeasible. Intersection of x + 2 y = 20 and y = 8 : x = 4 ⇒ ( 4 , 8 ) Other vertices: ( 0 , 0 ) , ( 15 , 0 ) , ( 20 , 0 ) , ( 0 , 8 ) — check feasibility: ( 0 , 0 ) : x + y = 0 ≤ 15 , x + 2 y = 0 ≤ 20 , y = 0 ≤ 8 ⇒ feasible. ( 15 , 0 ) : x + y = 15 , x + 2 y = 15 ≤ 20 , y = 0 ≤ 8 ⇒ feasible. ( 20 , 0 ) : x + y = 20 > 15 ⇒ infeasible. ( 0 , 8 ) : x + 2 y = 16 > 15 ⇒ infeasible. Corner points of feasible region: ( 0 , 0 ) , ( 15 , 0 ) , ( 10 , 5 ) , ( 4 , 8 ) Evaluate Z = 3 x + 5 y : ( 0 , 0 ) → 0 ( 15 , 0 ) → 45 ( 10 , 5 ) → 30 + 25 = 55 ( 4 , 8 ) → 12 + 40 = 52 Answer: C) 55
questions.solution: Solution Find corner points of the feasible region: Intersection of x + 2 y = 20 and x + y = 15 : subtract ⇒ y = 5 , x = 10 ⇒ ( 10 , 5 ) Intersection of x + y = 15 and y = 8 : x = 7 ⇒ ( 7 , 8 ) — check feasibility: x + 2 y = 7 + 16 = 23 > 20 ⇒ infeasible. Intersection of x + 2 y = 20 and y = 8 : x = 4 ⇒ ( 4 , 8 ) Other vertices: ( 0 , 0 ) , ( 15 , 0 ) , ( 20 , 0 ) , ( 0 , 8 ) — check feasibility: ( 0 , 0 ) : x + y = 0 ≤ 15 , x + 2 y = 0 ≤ 20 , y = 0 ≤ 8 ⇒ feasible. ( 15 , 0 ) : x + y = 15 , x + 2 y = 15 ≤ 20 , y = 0 ≤ 8 ⇒ feasible. ( 20 , 0 ) : x + y = 20 > 15 ⇒ infeasible. ( 0 , 8 ) : x + 2 y = 16 > 15 ⇒ infeasible. Corner points of feasible region: ( 0 , 0 ) , ( 15 , 0 ) , ( 10 , 5 ) , ( 4 , 8 ) Evaluate Z = 3 x + 5 y : ( 0 , 0 ) → 0 ( 15 , 0 ) → 45 ( 10 , 5 ) → 30 + 25 = 55 ( 4 , 8 ) → 12 + 40 = 52 Answer: C) 55
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Find corner points of the feasible region:

Intersection of x+2y=20 and x+y=15: subtract ⇒y=5,x=10 ⇒(10,5)
Intersection of x+y=15 and y=8: x=7 ⇒(7,8) — check feasibility: x+2y=7+16=23>20 ⇒ infeasible.
Intersection of x+2y=20 and y=8: x=4 ⇒(4,8)
Other vertices: (0,0), (15,0), (20,0), (0,8) — check feasibility:
(0,0): x+y=0≤15 , x+2y=0≤20 , y=0≤8 ⇒ feasible.
(15,0): x+y=15 , x+2y=15≤20 , y=0≤8 ⇒ feasible.
(20,0): x+y=20>15 ⇒ infeasible.
(0,8): x+2y=16>15 ⇒ infeasible.


Corner points of feasible region:

(0,0), (15,0), (10,5), (4,8)

Evaluate Z=3x+5y:
(0,0)→0
(15,0)→45
(10,5)→30+25=55
(4,8)→12+40=52

Answer: C) 55

questions.questionHtml: The equation of the plane passing through the lines x-41=y-31=z-22 and x-31=y-2-4=z5 is
questions.solutionText: Solution Line 1 passes through ( 4 , 3 , 2 ) with direction b 1 → = i ^ + j ^ + 2 k ^ . Line 2 passes through ( 3 , 2 , 0 ) with direction b 2 → = i ^ − 4 j ^ + 5 k ^ . Normal to plane = b 1 → × b 2 → : n → = | i ^ j ^ k ^ 1 1 2 1 − 4 5 | = i ^ ( 5 + 8 ) − j ^ ( 5 − 2 ) + k ^ ( − 4 − 1 ) = 13 i ^ − 3 j ^ − 5 k ^ Plane passes through ( 4 , 3 , 2 ) : 13 ( x − 4 ) − 3 ( y − 3 ) − 5 ( z − 2 ) = 0 13 x − 52 − 3 y + 9 − 5 z + 10 = 0 13 x − 3 y − 5 z = 33 Check if ( 3 , 2 , 0 ) lies on it: 39 − 6 − 0 = 33 13 x − 3 y − 5 z = 33 does not match any of options (1)-(3). Answer: Option (4) None of these

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Line 1 passes through (4,3,2) with direction b1→=i^+j^+2k^.
Line 2 passes through (3,2,0) with direction b2→=i^−4j^+5k^.

Normal to plane =b1→×b2→:
n→=|i^j^k^1121−45|
=i^(5+8)−j^(5−2)+k^(−4−1)
=13i^−3j^−5k^

Plane passes through (4,3,2):
13(x−4)−3(y−3)−5(z−2)=0
13x−52−3y+9−5z+10=0
13x−3y−5z=33

Check if (3,2,0) lies on it: 39−6−0=33 ✓

13x−3y−5z=33 does not match any of options (1)-(3).

Answer: Option (4) None of these
